metadata.title: Why the channel that returns tool results also carries the mistakes
metadata.topic: Error messages as in-loop self-correction signals
metadata.purpose: `tool_error` is a field on the same message type as results, mutually exclusive with `tool_outputs` and using the identical turn-taking rhythm — so error handling is architecturally the same loop as result handling, no special case.
metadata.clock: narration (Kokoro af_kore) — master clock; conform each beat to measured audio
metadata.still_lanes: c2v (success dict vs. error dict → corrected call)
metadata.exclusions: retry limits, backoff, exceptions outside the loop, the model's internal reasoning
metadata.visual_object: Two side-by-side `tool_outputs` dicts — one with results and `tool_error=None`, one with `tool_outputs=None` and an error string — then the corrected call that follows.
metadata.hook: The model requests a tool but omits a required parameter; the framework can't run it — yet the conversation doesn't deadlock or crash.

beats.narration_text: The model calls `perform_addition(a=9)` with no `b`; the system returns a `tool_outputs` message with `tool_outputs=None` and `tool_error="Missing required parameter 'b'"`; the model reads it and reissues `perform_addition(a=9, b=1)`.
beats.scaffold_note: from Key case — cold open on the concrete instance

beats.narration_text: If the error path returned a different shape than the success path, the model would need two mental models for responses — yet it self-corrects seamlessly; why?
The model requests a tool but omits a required parameter; the framework can't run it — yet the conversation doesn't deadlock or crash.

`tool_error` is a field on the same message type as results, mutually exclusive with `tool_outputs` and using the identical turn-taking rhythm — so error handling is architecturally the same loop as result handling, no special case.
beats.visual: Two side-by-side `tool_outputs` dicts — one with results and `tool_error=None`, one with `tool_outputs=None` and an error string — then the corrected call that follows.
beats.scaffold_note: the resolving mechanism; orbits the visual object

beats.narration_text: `perform_addition(a=9)` → `{tool_outputs:None, tool_error:"Missing required parameter 'b'"}` → next message `perform_addition(a=9, b=1)`.

`tool_error` is a field on the same message type as results, mutually exclusive with `tool_outputs` and using the identical turn-taking rhythm — so error handling is architecturally the same loop as result handling, no special case.
